channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: After Nick Bosa celebration, ‘Trump dance’ takes sports world by storm
item.description: Days after Trump's reelection, the 49ers defensive end celebrated a sack in the team's win over Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays say new St. Pete stadium is unlikely to be ready for 2028 season, if at all
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — A combination of severe hurricane damage to Tropicana Field and political delays on financing means it is highly unlikely the Tampa Bay Rays' planned new stadium will be ready for the 2028 season, if at all, the team said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cleveland's Stephen Vogt wins AL Manager of the Year in first season as skipper at any level
item.description: Cleveland’s Stephen Vogt won American League Manager of the Year honors on Tuesday night after leading the Guardians to a division title and a 92-win season in his first year as a skipper at any level.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kickers are as good as ever these days, yet some of the NFL's true standouts are suddenly struggling
item.description: OWINGS MILLS, Md. (AP) — This is supposed to be a golden age for placekicking in the NFL. Try telling that to the fans in Baltimore, Philadelphia and Cincinnati right now.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NHL referee Mitch Dunning is home and expects to make a full recovery, league says
item.description: The NHL referee who was stretchered out of a game following a violent collision with Colorado defenseman Josh Manson was back home and expected to make a full recovery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gerardo 'Tata' Martino stepping down as coach of Lionel Messi's Inter Miami, AP source says
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Gerardo “Tata” Martino, who guided Inter Miami to the best regular season record in Major League Soccer history this season, is resigning as coach of the club, a person with knowledge of the decision said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spurs star Wembanyama will miss game against Thunder as he recovers from bruised right knee
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama will not play against the Oklahoma City Thunder on Tuesday night as he recovers from a bruised right knee. San Antonio guard Devin Vassell was listed as questionable for the NBA Cup game with a sore left knee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USU seeks to overturn loss in forfeited volleyball game against San Jose State
item.description: Just hours after Utah lawmakers urged Utah State University to join a Title IX lawsuit against the Mountain West Conference, the Logan-based school filed a motion to join the lawsuit, in part.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Jets fire general manager Joe Douglas as woeful season drags on
item.description: The New York Jets' attempt to salvage another season has resulted in another major shakeup with reports indicating Tuesday that general manager Joe Douglas was fired more than halfway through his sixth season with the organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ESPN star getting 'very, very worried' about Jerry Jones, has Joe Biden in mind
item.description: ESPN star Stephen A. Smith expressed concerns for Dallas Cowboys owner Jerry Jones after the team's loss to the Houston Texans on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Analysis: Some NBA teams know it's time to shake out of early-season slides — or else
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Postgame interviews in the NBA typically start about 10 minutes after the final buzzer. Coaches usually speak first, followed by a few players. When a team wins, most people are in a great mood. When a team loses, not so much. That's the normal routine.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texans never trail while handing Cowboys 5th straight loss, 34-10
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Derek Barnett forced the first fumble and returned the second for a touchdown on the definitive defensive play of the game for the Houston Texans. Joe Mixon did his thing, and C.J. Stroud didn’t have to do much to extend the misery of the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: BYU suffers first loss of season to Kansas, 17-13
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU still has a great shot at the Big 12 Championship Game, but the Cougars undefeated season came to an end Saturday night against Kansas. Jalon Daniels threw for 169 yards, Dylan Neal ran for two touchdowns and Kansas upset No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jump around: Cavaliers improve to 14-0, celebrate by dancing with frog mascot
item.description: They continued their perfect start, improving to 14-0 on Friday night with a 144-126 win over the Chicago Bulls in an NBA Cup opener that served as another showcase for a Cavs team clicking like never before.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SJSU volleyball captain demands removal of transgender athlete
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (KRON) -- Some San Jose State University women's volleyball players are suing their head coach, their university, and the Division-1 Mountain West Conference for allowing a transgender player to be on the team.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz beat Dallas for first home victory, 115-113
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was finally home sweet home for the Utah Jazz. John Collins scored the last of his 28 points on a tiebreaking dunk with 6.4 seconds left to give the Utah Jazz their first home victory of the season, 115-113 over the Dallas Mavericks on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Curry upset after Klay 'ghosted' him before game, Draymond Green says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- While it seemed to be all sunshine and roses inside Chase Center as the packed arena welcomed back Klay Thompson on Tuesday, that wasn't exactly the case -- at least amongst the players on the court.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Bears place Jaquan Brisker on injured reserve
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears placed safety Jaquan Brisker on injured reserve Thursday, team officials announced.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Sports Network launching subscription streaming service
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago Sports Network is launching a streaming service Friday amid its negotiations with Xfinity. The subscription service will start at $19.99 for one team or $29.99 for full access to CHSN. A 7-day free trial is being offered.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Olympic champion Lindsey Vonn is ending her retirement at age 40 to make a skiing comeback
item.description: Vonn's last competition came in February 2019, after she dealt with a long series of injuries throughout her career.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coby White sinks 3 FTs in final seconds, Bulls avoid close call at buzzer to hang on vs. Knicks after blowing 22-point lead
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It came down to a matter of inches at Madison Square Garden for the Bulls on Wednesday night. This time, it went their way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Collins scores 29, but Jazz fall to Suns, 120-112
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Jazz got big contributions from John Collins and rookie Kyle Filipowski, but it wasn't enough to slow down the Phoenix Suns Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Klay Thompson returns to cheers from some 400 Warriors employees
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Klay Thompson exited the team bus then beamed as he strolled back into Chase Center on Tuesday night, greeted by some 400 cheering Warriors employees who lined up along his path to the Dallas locker room to show their love and appreciation for the former Golden State star.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utes quarterback Brandon Rose out for the season
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In a year that has been devasted by season-ending injuries, the Utes football team has suffered another one. After making his first career start, quarterback Brandon Rose has been lost for the year after suffering a lower leg injury in Saturday's 22-21 loss to BYU.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU ranked 6th in College Football Playoff poll
item.description: PROVO, Utah (AB4 Sports) - It was one of the most thrilling victories in BYU history, and will go down as a true rivalry classic. But perhaps what was even more thrilling for the Cougars than their 22-21 win over Utah Saturday night, was the reception they received late Saturday night in Provo.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 18-year-old arrested for throwing water bottle at football game between U of U, BYU
item.description: An 18-year-old was arrested after reportedly throwing a water bottle onto the field during the BYU-Utah game on Nov. 9.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
